chunks.text: Plotting Real Time Audio Plotting Real Time Audio The applet below combines Gabriele Bulfon's Playing Wave applet and the PlotLive Ptplot class. The PlayingWave.java sources show how we added a PlotLive widget. This demo is not very polished, we di

ive widget. This demo is not very polished, we did it so that we could experiment with multithreaded applets. This demo simulates a plucked string sound using the Karplus-Strong algorithm. The user can control the various features of the new sound,

can control the various features of the new sound, and then mouse on the action button, which will create the new sound. The user controlled features are: Replace / Add This widget determines whether the new sound replaces the old sound or adds to t

the new sound replaces the old sound or adds to the old sound. Random / Sine This widget determines whether the new sound will be a random wave, or a sine wave. Text entry widget This widget is used to set the period of the sine wave. Action button

to set the period of the sine wave. Action button This button causes the new sound to be generated Damp slider and entry widget This controls the damping. If the value is more than 50 , then the sound gets louder, if the value is less than 50 , it g

d gets louder, if the value is less than 50 , it gets softer. Note that the wave plot on the left side is mouse sensitive. If you depress and hold the left mouse button while over the signal, you can drag the signal around. For more information abo

n drag the signal around. For more information about this applet, see the Playing Wave webpage Up to the Example index - Back to a Plot of 10,000 binary points Copyright © 1997, The Regents of the University of California. All rights reserved. Last u
